statements.rationale: The loader delegates to a statically referenced helper that reaches across databases, so the writer remains identifiable but the profile stays partial.

refactor.extracted_sql: -- Cross-database delegation: This procedure delegates to [ArchiveDB].[silver].[usp_stage_DimCrossDbProfile] -- The actual transformation logic is opaque, but based on source table (bronze.employee) and target columns, -- this SELECT represents the logical structure of data flowing to silver.DimCrossDbProfile SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY EmployeeKey) AS CrossDbProfileKey, AlternateKey AS EmployeeAlternateKey, Title AS EmployeeTitle FROM bronze.employee
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT EmployeeKey, AlternateKey, Title FROM bronze.employee ), logical AS ( SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY EmployeeKey) AS CrossDbProfileKey, AlternateKey AS EmployeeAlternateKey, Title AS EmployeeTitle FROM import ), final AS ( SELECT CrossDbProfileKey, EmployeeAlternateKey, EmployeeTitle FROM logical ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both SQLs access bronze.employee as the sole source table.

output_columns.summary: Both SQLs return identical columns: CrossDbProfileKey (INT), EmployeeAlternateKey (NVARCHAR(15)), EmployeeTitle (NVARCHAR(50)).

joins.summary: No joins present in either SQL.

filters.summary: No WHERE clauses or filters present in either SQL.

aggregation_grain.summary: Both SQLs apply the same window function ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY EmployeeKey) to generate CrossDbProfileKey. Grain is preserved.
